Full night's rest after a calm, productive Sunday; Olga sleeps well on quiet evenings and wakes naturally in the early morning.

diary:
Sunday was one of those perfect Seattle days—72 degrees, not a cloud in sight. I woke early to check on my batch downstairs; the homebrew's coming along nicely, should be ready in another week or two. The light rail ride downtown was peaceful, barely anyone on it at that hour.

Pike Place was bustling but in that easy, weekend way. Lots of families picking up flowers for their dinner tables, couples browsing. Mrs. Chen came by like she always does, got her usual pink peonies. At Matt's I ran into an old friend I haven't seen in months—we talked longer than I meant to over coffee and a bagel.

Back at the stall the afternoon drifted by. I could hear the lake traffic, all those boats out enjoying the sun. By the time I headed home, I felt that Sunday tiredness settling in—the good kind. Made a simple pasta dinner, played some chess online, and spent time with my brewing project. There's something grounding about a day that just... works.
thought_process: Today felt like a gift, honestly. The weather put me in a reflective mood from the start—nothing urgent, nowhere I needed to be but where I was. I lingered with customers more than usual because they seemed to want it, and the market had that unhurried Sunday energy. Running into my friend was serendipitous; it reminded me I should reach out to people more. The calm didn't make me restless like it sometimes does; instead it let me settle into my routines—the homebrew, the chess, the small rituals. I think I needed this kind of day after a week of managing inventory and dealing with suppliers. By evening I felt genuinely content, not just tired.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
